name: David Sánchez
abstract: David Sánchez (born 3 September 1968 in Guaynabo, Puerto Rico) is a Grammy Award-winning jazz tenor saxophonist from Puerto Rico.

concerts.label: JVC Jazz Festival: Salsa Jam: Tribute to Tito Puente
concerts.venue: Isaac Stern Auditorium / Ronald O. Perelman Stage

about_quotes.speaker: Harry Allen
about_quotes.evidence: David Sanchez was there, Michael Mossman, Ralph Bowen, Steve Nelson, and there was a bunch of others. There was a whole slew of them. Most everybody that was there has gone on to make it in the business.
about_quotes.doc_title: Harry Allen interviewed by Monk Rowe, Chautauqua, New York, September 13, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: James Moody
about_quotes.evidence: And I've got Antonio Heart, David Sanchez along with the other guys. [...] Dizzy put together the whole concept of the United Nations Band. And then that band became a reality and you were a part of it.
about_quotes.doc_title: James Moody — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Benny Golson
about_quotes.evidence: The night I played, it was David Sanchez – the tenor player – me, and Dizzy of course... We played that night
about_quotes.doc_title: Benny Golson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Melba Liston
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] But people like – we heard David Sanchez. He reached back to a lot of guys from Bird on up.
about_quotes.doc_title: Melba Liston — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Kenny Barron
about_quotes.doc_title: Kenny Barron — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
